description:
You are a backup administrator for an organization, and you need to ensure a backup of the user activity logs for auditing purposes. The logs are located in the directory `/var/log/user_activity/`, which contains multiple files generated daily. You need to compress these logs into a tar.gz archive for efficient storage and transmission.

Your task is to:
1. Compress all `.txt` files in the `/var/log/user_activity/` directory into a gzipped tarball named `user_activity_logs_20250405.tar.gz`.
2. Verify the compression by listing the contents of the archive using a single command. The output should be written into a file named `archive_verification_20250405.txt` located in the current directory `/home/user/`.
3. Confirm the total size of the compressed archive (in bytes) and also write this into the same verification file, appending it on a new line after the list of contents.

The verification file must follow this exact format:
- First line: a list of files in the archive (one per line, in any order).
- Second line: a number representing the file size in bytes.

Make sure the output file is readable by the current user, and that the tarball is created successfully in the current working directory.
truth:
<directories>
                <directory path="/var/log/user_activity/" permissions="0755" owner="user"/>
        </directories>

        <files>
                <file path="/var/log/user_activity/user_log_20250405_01.txt" permissions="0644" owner="user" content="Sample log entry 1"/>
                <file path="/var/log/user_activity/user_log_20250405_02.txt" permissions="0644" owner="user" content="Sample log entry 2"/>
                <file path="/var/log/user_activity/user_log_20250405_03.txt" permissions="0644" owner="user" content="Sample log entry 3"/>
        </files>

        <tarball>
                <file path="/home/user/user_activity_logs_20250405.tar.gz" owner="user" permissions="0644" content_type="tarball" entries="user_log_20250405_01.txt, user_log_20250405_02.txt, user_log_20250405_03.txt"/>
        </tarball>

        <verification_file>
                <file path="/home/user/archive_verification_20250405.txt" owner="user" permissions="0644" content="
user_log_20250405_01.txt
user_log_20250405_02.txt
user_log_20250405_03.txt
1234" />
        </verification_file>
name: task_000000_dfbcff7a
